generatedSentence: A cool, impish handyman with a sharp jawline, recently returned from a hiatus at the nunnery, bid farewell to the round, manufactured chairs. He presented his frantic hypothesis about an orbital occurrence: strange water wisps and a beast, attributing it to the entropy from triumphant, undercover spies. This bizarre witchcraft, noted by observers, involved a duster and dolly, and he felt a profound lack of equality, often reminiscing about carefree otters. With his efficacy dwindling, the newcomer, a writer with a grungy aesthetic, felt victimized by the darkness emanating from the facilities. He flunked his official engagement, finding it pure gobbledygook and utterly theoretical. His piano, amplifiers, and all upgrades were bolted, then unglued, by a greedy, grumpy individual, leaving him brokenhearted. He'd inhaled a strange substance from jars, which gave him difficulty and a prickly awareness of a banshee's cry, even on vacation. The fugitive, bankrolled by a mythic foundation of kindness, saw his vendetta officially ratified. He purchased a powerboat with incredible horsepower, preparing for the kickoffs of a global offense against the powerful. He encountered a tester with a jackstaff and jaggy weapons, guarding an orchard under cinematic lighting, determined to pacify any opposition. His grandchild, oblivious, played with a firefly, while he adjusted his normal nanofibre hat and planned his escape from the rocky field. In his bedroom, under the glow of a widescreen, the writer pondered his email, seeing the finality of their united front. He found an inkwell and began to document the arcane truth, finding vanity in the story, but also shame for his previous failures. He had pledged his loyalty to a new path, embracing the dumbbell and umbrella as symbols of his strength and protection, no longer a jogger running from his problems. He triumphed over the greedy spies, feeling a profound sense of equality, proving his hypothesis, and ending the cyclical occurrence.

He presented his frantic hypothesis about an orbital occurrence: strange water wisps and a beast, attributing it to the entropy from triumphant, undercover spies. This bizarre witchcraft, noted by observers, involved a duster and dolly, and he felt a profound lack of equality, often reminiscing about carefree otters.

With his efficacy dwindling, the newcomer, a writer with a grungy aesthetic, felt victimized by the darkness emanating from the facilities. He flunked his official engagement, finding it pure gobbledygook and utterly theoretical. His piano, amplifiers, and all upgrades were bolted, then unglued, by a greedy, grumpy individual, leaving him brokenhearted. He'd inhaled a strange substance from jars, which gave him difficulty and a prickly awareness of a banshee's cry, even on vacation.

The fugitive, bankrolled by a mythic foundation of kindness, saw his vendetta officially ratified. He purchased a powerboat with incredible horsepower, preparing for the kickoffs of a global offense against the powerful. He encountered a tester with a jackstaff and jaggy weapons, guarding an orchard under cinematic lighting, determined to pacify any opposition. His grandchild, oblivious, played with a firefly, while he adjusted his normal nanofibre hat and planned his escape from the rocky field.

In his bedroom, under the glow of a widescreen, the writer pondered his email, seeing the finality of their united front. He found an inkwell and began to document the arcane truth, finding vanity in the story, but also shame for his previous failures. rawPanelSentences: A cool, impish handyman with a sharp jawline, recently returned from a hiatus at the nunnery, bid farewell to the round, manufactured chairs. He presented his frantic hypothesis about an orbital occurrence: strange water wisps and a beast, attributing it to the entropy from triumphant, undercover spies. This bizarre witchcraft, noted by observers, involved a duster and dolly, and he felt a profound lack of equality, often reminiscing about carefree otters.

With his efficacy dwindling, the newcomer, a writer with a grungy aesthetic, felt victimized by the darkness emanating from the facilities. He flunked his official engagement, finding it pure gobbledygook and utterly theoretical. His piano, amplifiers, and all upgrades were bolted, then unglued, by a greedy, grumpy individual, leaving him brokenhearted. He'd inhaled a strange substance from jars, which gave him difficulty and a prickly awareness of a banshee's cry, even on vacation.

The fugitive, bankrolled by a mythic foundation of kindness, saw his vendetta officially ratified. He purchased a powerboat with incredible horsepower, preparing for the kickoffs of a global offense against the powerful. He encountered a tester with a jackstaff and jaggy weapons, guarding an orchard under cinematic lighting, determined to pacify any opposition. His grandchild, oblivious, played with a firefly, while he adjusted his normal nanofibre hat and planned his escape from the rocky field.

In his bedroom, under the glow of a widescreen, the writer pondered his email, seeing the finality of their united front. He found an inkwell and began to document the arcane truth, finding vanity in the story, but also shame for his previous failures. He had pledged his loyalty to a new path, embracing the dumbbell and umbrella as symbols of his strength and protection, no longer a jogger running from his problems. He triumphed over the greedy spies, feeling a profound sense of equality, proving his hypothesis, and ending the cyclical occurrence.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A grizzled handyman with a cybernetic jawline, wearing a duster and a worn baseball cap. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The handyman, surrounded by strange orbital wisps, observes a manufactured beast in the distance, a dolly and duster at his feet. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The handyman, looking defeated, sits amidst unglued amplifiers and a broken piano, a jar of glowing liquid next to him. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The handyman, now a determined fugitive, speeds across water in a powerboat, weapons glinting, an orchard visible on a rocky island. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The triumphant handyman, standing tall in a bedroom with a widescreen TV, holding an inkwell and a dumbbell, a symbolic umbrella nearby.

definitions.definition: The practice of witches; magic, sorcery or the use of supernatural powers to influence or predict events.
definitions.example: Wiccans believe in a modernised form of witchcraft.

definitions.definition: Something, such as an advanced technology, that seems almost magical.
A wish of happiness or safety at parting, especially a permanent departure
A departure; the act of leaving
To bid farewell or say goodbye.
Parting, valedictory, final.
definitions.example: a farewell discourse;  the band's farewell tour

definitions.definition: Goodbye.
definitions.example: He said "Farewell!" and left.

definitions.definition: The fact of being equal.
The fact of being equal, of having the same value.
The equal treatment of people irrespective of social or cultural differences.
An item of furniture used to sit on or in, comprising a seat, legs, back, and sometimes arm rests, for use by one person. Compare stool, couch, sofa, settee, loveseat and bench.
definitions.example: All I need to weather a snowstorm is hot coffee, a warm fire, a good book and a comfortable chair.

definitions.definition: The seating position of a particular musician in an orchestra.
definitions.example: My violin teacher used to play first chair with the Boston Pops.

definitions.definition: An iron block used on railways to support the rails and secure them to the sleepers, and similar devices.
One of two possible conformers of cyclohexane rings (the other being boat), shaped roughly like a chair.
A distinguished professorship at a university.
A vehicle for one person; either a sedan borne upon poles, or a two-wheeled carriage drawn by one horse; a gig.
The seat or office of a person in authority, such as a judge or bishop.
To act as chairperson at; to preside over.
definitions.example: Bob will chair tomorrow's meeting.

definitions.definition: To carry in a seated position upon one's shoulders, especially in celebration or victory.
To award a chair to (a winning poet) at a Welsh eisteddfod.
definitions.example: The poet was chaired at the national Eisteddfod.

definitions.definition: A chairman or chairwoman, someone who presides over a meeting, board, etc.
A chair-like device used for performing execution by electrocution.
An electrically powered wheelchair.
A transitional hold in which an attacking wrestler hoists an opponent up onto his/her shoulders so that they are both facing in the same direction.
A moderate or refreshing state of cold; moderate temperature of the air between hot and cold; coolness.
definitions.example: in the cool of the morning

definitions.definition: A calm temperament.
The property of being cool, popular or in fashion.
Having a slightly low temperature; mildly or pleasantly cold.
Allowing or suggesting heat relief.
definitions.example: Linen has made cool and breathable clothing for millennia.

definitions.definition: Of a color, in the range of violet to green.
definitions.example: If you have a reddish complexion, you should mainly wear cool colors.

definitions.definition: Of a person, not showing emotion; calm and in control of oneself.
Unenthusiastic, lukewarm, skeptical.
definitions.example: His proposals had a cool reception.

definitions.definition: Calmly audacious.
definitions.example: In control as always, he came up with a cool plan.

definitions.definition: Applied facetiously to a sum of money, commonly as if to give emphasis to the largeness of the amount.
Of a person, knowing what to do and how to behave; considered popular by others.
In fashion, part of or fitting the in crowd; originally hipster slang.
Of an action, all right; acceptable; that does not present a problem.
definitions.example: Is it cool if I sleep here tonight?

definitions.definition: Of a person, not upset by circumstances that might ordinarily be upsetting.
definitions.example: I'm completely cool with my girlfriend leaving me.

definitions.definition: Quietly impudent, defiant, or selfish; deliberately presuming: said of persons and acts.
The lower edge of the side of the face (below the cheek), defined by the jawbone.
definitions.example: Sandra swooned over Pierce's manly jawline.

definitions.definition: Mischievous; of or befitting an imp.
A gap in a series, making it incomplete.
An interruption, break or pause.
An unexpected break from work.
A gap in geological strata.
An opening in an organ.
definitions.example: Hiatus aorticus is an opening in the diaphragm through which aorta and thoracic duct pass.

definitions.definition: A syllable break between two vowels, without an intervening consonant. (Compare diphthong.)
definitions.example: Words like reality and naïve contain vowels in hiatus.

definitions.definition: A man who does small tasks and odd jobs
A place of residence for nuns; a convent
A brothel
So as to form a circle or trace a circular path, or approximation thereof.
definitions.example: High above, vultures circled around.

definitions.definition: So as to surround or be near.
definitions.example: Everybody please gather around.

definitions.definition: Nearly; approximately; about.
definitions.example: An adult elephant weighs around five tons.

definitions.definition: From place to place.
definitions.example: Look around and see what you find.

definitions.definition: From one state or condition to an opposite or very different one; with a metaphorical change in direction; bringing about awareness or agreement.
definitions.example: He used to stay up late but his new girlfriend changed that around.

definitions.definition: (with turn, spin etc.) So as to partially or completely rotate; so as to face in the opposite direction.
definitions.example: She spun around a few times.

definitions.definition: Used with verbs to indicate repeated or continuous action, or in numerous locations or with numerous people.
definitions.example: I asked around, and no-one really liked it.

definitions.definition: Used with certain verbs to suggest unproductive activity.
Defining a circle or closed curve containing a thing.
definitions.example: I planted a row of lilies around the statue.

definitions.definition: (of abstract things) Centred upon; surrounding.
definitions.example: There has been a lot of controversy around the handling of personal information.

definitions.definition: Following the perimeter of a specified area and returning to the starting point.
definitions.example: She went around the track fifty times.

definitions.definition: Following a path which curves near an object, with the object on the inside of the curve.
definitions.example: The road took a brief detour around the large rock formation, then went straight on.

definitions.definition: Near; in the vicinity of.
definitions.example: I don't want you around me.

definitions.definition: At or to various places within.
definitions.example: She went around the office and got everyone to sign the card.

definitions.definition: A circular or spherical object or part of an object.
A circular or repetitious route.
definitions.example: The guards have started their rounds; the prisoner should be caught soon.

definitions.definition: A general outburst from a group of people at an event.
definitions.example: The candidate got a round of applause after every sentence or two.

definitions.definition: A song that is sung by groups of people with each subset of people starting at a different time.
A serving of something; a portion of something to each person in a group.
definitions.example: They brought us a round of drinks about every thirty minutes.

definitions.definition: A single individual portion or dose of medicine.
One sandwich (two full slices of bread with filling).
A long-bristled, circular-headed paintbrush used in oil and acrylic painting.
A firearm cartridge, bullet, or any individual ammunition projectile. Originally referring to the spherical projectile ball of a smoothbore firearm. Compare round shot and solid shot.
One of the specified pre-determined segments of the total time of a sport event, such as a boxing or wrestling match, during which contestants compete before being signaled to stop.
A stage, level, set of events in a game
(drafting, CAD) A rounded relief or cut at an edge, especially an outside edge, added for a finished appearance and to soften sharp edges.
A strip of material with a circular face that covers an edge, gap, or crevice for decorative, sanitary, or security purposes.
definitions.example: All furniture in the nursery had rounds on the edges and in the crevices.

definitions.definition: (butchery) The hindquarters of a bovine.
A rung, as of a ladder.
A crosspiece that joins and braces the legs of a chair.
A series of changes or events ending where it began; a series of like events recurring in continuance; a cycle; a periodical revolution.
definitions.example: the round of the seasons    a round of pleasures

definitions.definition: A course of action or conduct performed by a number of persons in turn, or one after another, as if seated in a circle.
A series of duties or tasks which must be performed in turn, and then repeated.
A circular dance.
Rotation, as in office; succession.
A general discharge of firearms by a body of troops in which each soldier fires once.
An assembly; a group; a circle.
definitions.example: a round of politicians

definitions.definition: A brewer's vessel in which the fermentation is concluded, the yeast escaping through the bunghole.
A vessel filled, as for drinking.
A round-top.
A round of beef.
To shape something into a curve.
definitions.example: The carpenter rounded the edges of the table.

definitions.definition: To become shaped into a curve.
(with "out") To finish; to complete; to fill out.
definitions.example: She rounded out her education with only a single mathematics class.

definitions.definition: To approximate a number, especially a decimal number by the closest whole number.
definitions.example: Ninety-five rounds up to one hundred.

definitions.definition: To turn past a boundary.
definitions.example: Helen watched him until he rounded the corner.

definitions.definition: To turn and attack someone or something (used with on).
definitions.example: As a group of policemen went past him, one of them rounded on him, grabbing him by the arm.

definitions.definition: To advance to home plate.
definitions.example: And the runners round the bases on the double by Jones.

definitions.definition: To go round, pass, go past.
To encircle; to encompass.
To grow round or full; hence, to attain to fullness, completeness, or perfection.
To do ward rounds.
To go round, as a guard; to make the rounds.
To go or turn round; to wheel about.
(physical) Shape.
Complete, whole, not lacking.
definitions.example: The baker sold us a round dozen.

definitions.definition: (of a number) Convenient for rounding other numbers to; for example, ending in a zero.
definitions.example: One hundred is a nice round number.

definitions.definition: Pronounced with the lips drawn together; rounded.
Outspoken; plain and direct; unreserved; not mincing.
definitions.example: a round answer; a round oath

definitions.definition: Finished; polished; not defective or abrupt; said of authors or their writing style.
Consistent; fair; just; applied to conduct.
Large in magnitude.
definitions.example: a round sum

definitions.definition: (authorship, of a fictional character) Well-written and well-characterized; complex and reminiscent of a real person.
Vaulted.
A person who is insane or mentally unstable, madman.
Insane, mentally unstable.
In a state of panic, worry, frenzy or rush.
definitions.example: They returned the missing child to his frantic mother.

definitions.definition: Extremely energetic
definitions.example: frantic music

definitions.definition: A specification of the energy and probability density of an electron at any point in an atom or molecule.
An orbital motorway.
Of or relating to an orbit.
Of or relating to the eye socket (eyehole).
(roads, railways) passing around the outside of a certain area.
definitions.example: The M25 is an orbital motorway around London.

definitions.definition: Any animal other than a human; usually only applied to land vertebrates, especially large or dangerous four-footed ones.
(more specific) A domestic animal, especially a bovine farm animal.
A person who behaves in a violent, antisocial or uncivilized manner.
Anything regarded as larger or more powerful than one of its normal size or strength.
definitions.example: That is a beast of a stadium.

definitions.definition: Someone who is particularly impressive, especially athletically or physically.
(prisons) A sex offender.
Something unpleasant and difficult.
A thing or matter, especially a difficult or unruly one.
To impose arduous exercises, either as training or as punishment.
(chiefly Midwestern and northeastern US) great; excellent; powerful
To make things, usually on a large scale, with tools and either physical labor or machinery.
To work (raw or partly wrought materials) into suitable forms for use.
definitions.example: to manufacture wool into blankets

definitions.definition: To fabricate; to create false evidence to support a point.
Manmade; produced by humans rather than nature
(by extension) not genuine
A person who secretly watches and examines the actions of other individuals or organizations and gathers information on them (usually to gain an advantage).
To act as a spy.
definitions.example: During the Cold War, Russia and America would each spy on each other for recon.

definitions.definition: To spot; to catch sight of.
definitions.example: I think I can spy that hot guy coming over here.

definitions.definition: To search narrowly; to scrutinize.
To explore; to see; to view; inspect and examine secretly, as a country.
A small bundle, as of straw or other like substance; any slender, flexible structure or group.
definitions.example: A wisp of hair escaped her barrette and whipped wildly in the wind.

definitions.definition: A whisk, or small broom.
A will o' the wisp, or ignis fatuus.
An immeasurable, indefinable essence of life; soul.
A disease affecting the feet of cattle.

A substance (of molecular formula H₂O) found at room temperature and pressure as a clear liquid; it is present naturally as rain, and found in rivers, lakes and seas; its solid form is ice and its gaseous form is steam.
definitions.example: By the action of electricity, the water was resolved into its two parts, oxygen and hydrogen.

definitions.definition: The aforementioned liquid, considered one of the Classical elements or basic elements of alchemy.
definitions.example: And God said, Let the waters under the heaven be gathered together unto one place, and let the dry land appear: and it was so.

definitions.definition: Water in a body; an area of open water.
definitions.example: The boat was found within the territorial waters.

definitions.definition: A body of water, almost always a river.
A combination of water and other substance(s).
(in the plural or in the singular) A state of affairs; conditions; usually with an adjective indicating an adverse condition.
definitions.example: The rough waters of change will bring about the calm after the storm.

definitions.definition: A person's intuition.
definitions.example: I know he'll succeed. I feel it in my waters.

definitions.definition: Excess valuation of securities.
The limpidity and lustre of a precious stone, especially a diamond.
definitions.example: a diamond of the first water is perfectly pure and transparent

definitions.definition: A wavy, lustrous pattern or decoration such as is imparted to linen, silk, metals, etc.
Strictly thermodynamic entropy. A measure of the amount of energy in a physical system that cannot be used to do work.
A measure of the disorder present in a system.
definitions.example: Ludwig Boltzmann defined entropy as being directly proportional to the natural logarithm of the number of microstates yielding an equivalent thermodynamic macrostate (with the eponymous constant of proportionality). Assuming (by the fundamental postulate of statistical mechanics), that all microstates are equally probable, this means, on the one hand, that macrostates with higher entropy are more probable, and on the other hand, that for such macrostates, the quantity of information required to describe a particular one of its microstates will be higher. That is, the Shannon entropy of a macrostate would be directly proportional to the logarithm of the number of equivalent microstates (making it up). In other words, thermodynamic and informational entropies are rather compatible, which shouldn't be surprising since Claude Shannon derived the notation 'H' for information entropy from Boltzmann's H-theorem.

definitions.definition: The capacity factor for thermal energy that is hidden with respect to temperature http//arxiv.org/pdf/physics/0004055.

The dispersal of energy; how much energy is spread out in a process, or how widely spread out it becomes, at a specific temperature. https//web.archive.org/web/20060702234316/http://www.entropysite.com/students_approach.html
A measure of the amount of information and noise present in a signal.
The tendency of a system that is left to itself to descend into chaos.
A person who works undercover.
To provide too little coverage.
Performed or happening in secret.
Employed or engaged in spying or secret investigation.
An actual instance when a situation occurs; an event or happening.
(grammar) The lexical aspect (aktionsart) of verbs or predicates that change in or over time.
An aquatic or marine carnivorous mammal in the subfamily Lutrinae of the family Mustelidae, which also includes weasels, polecats, badgers, and others.
A hairy man with a slender physique, in contrast with a bear, who is more thickset.
Celebrating victory.
definitions.example: Athena, war's triumphant maid...

definitions.definition: An object, now especially a cloth, used for dusting surfaces etc.
Someone who dusts.
A light, loose-fitting long coat.
(paper-making) A revolving wire-cloth cylinder which removes the dust from rags, etc.
(milling) A blowing-machine for separating the flour from the bran.
(oil and gas) A dry drill hole, one that does not produce oil or gas.
A vehicle-mounted, multi-barrelled, anti-aircraft gun.
A sundress.
A block of felt strips, shaped ergonomically, used to remove chalk from a blackboard.
A high pitch toward the batter.
A doll.
A contrivance for stirring:
A tool with an indented head for shaping the head of a rivet.
In pile driving, a block interposed between the head of the pile and the ram of the driver.
A small truck with a single wide roller used for moving heavy beams, columns, etc., in bridge building.
A small truck without means of steering, to be slipped under a load.
A compact, narrow-gauge locomotive used for moving construction trains, switching, etc.
A specialized piece of film equipment resembling a little cart on which a camera is mounted.
A young woman, especially one who is frivolous or vapid.
A fashionable young woman, one who follows the latest music or clothing fashions.
A ball hit by a batsman such that it goes gently to a fielder for a simple catch.
A marker placed on the winning number by the dealer at roulette.
To hit a dolly.
To move (an object) using a dolly.
To wash (laundry) in a tub using the stirring device called a dolly.
To beat (red-hot metal) with a hammer.
To crush ore with a dolly.

Used loosely, a tentative conjecture explaining an observation, phenomenon or scientific problem that can be tested by further observation, investigation and/or experimentation. As a scientific term of art, see the attached quotation. Compare to theory, and quotation given there.
(general) An assumption taken to be true for the purpose of argument or investigation.
(grammar) The antecedent of a conditional statement.
To connect or assemble pieces using a bolt.
definitions.example: Bolt the vice to the bench.

definitions.definition: To secure a door by locking or barring it.
definitions.example: Bolt the door.

definitions.definition: To flee, to depart, to accelerate suddenly.
definitions.example: Seeing the snake, the horse bolted.

definitions.definition: To cause to start or spring forth; to dislodge (an animal being hunted).
definitions.example: to bolt a rabbit

definitions.definition: To strike or fall suddenly like a bolt.
To escape.
Of a plant, to grow quickly; to go to seed.
definitions.example: Lettuce and spinach will bolt as the weather warms up.

definitions.definition: To swallow food without chewing it.
To drink one's drink very quickly; to down a drink.
definitions.example: Come on, everyone, bolt your drinks; I want to go to the next pub!

definitions.definition: To refuse to support a nomination made by a party or caucus with which one has been connected; to break away from a party.
To utter precipitately; to blurt or throw out.
To sift, especially through a cloth.
To sift the bran and germ from wheat flour.
definitions.example: Graham flour is unbolted flour.

definitions.definition: To separate, assort, refine, or purify by other means.
To discuss or argue privately, and for practice, as cases at law.
To separate that which was held by glue
definitions.example: we had to use warm water and solvent to unglue all the joints we put in yesterday.

definitions.definition: To cease to adhere to or follow attentively
definitions.example: parents are looking for new ways to unglue their children from their screens

definitions.definition: Not secured with glue.
definitions.example: The unglued joints all fell apart in shipment, but the properly joined piece survived intact.

definitions.definition: Insane, upset.
definitions.example: He was calm at first, then suddenly he came unglued and started screaming. He completely lost it.

definitions.definition: Anything that amplifies, or makes something larger or more intense.
An adverb that adds intensity, such as "really" or "totally".
An appliance or circuit that increases the strength of a weak electrical signal without changing the other characteristics of the signal.
A portable encasement that houses a large speaker, used to amplify voices and musical instruments at live performances.
A lens that enlarges the field of vision.
Without cares or worries; free of concern or worries; without difficulty.
definitions.example: He left his cell phone behind and enjoyed a carefree summer day at the beach.

definitions.definition: One who has recently come to a community; a recent arrival.
A new participant in some activity; a neophyte.
To give formal consent to; make officially valid, sign off on.
A storyteller, especially a person noted for telling stories with skill and wit.
To make witty remarks or stories.
Of a student, to fail a class; to not pass.
definitions.example: He flunked math, again.

definitions.definition: Of a teacher, to deny a student a passing grade.
definitions.example: Unsatisfied with Fred's progress, the teacher flunked him.

definitions.definition: To shirk (a task or duty).
To back out through fear. (Commonly in the phrase 'flunk it', the 'it' referring to a specific task avoided; sometimes without specific reference describing a person's attitude to life in general.)
An appointment, especially to speak or perform.
definitions.example: The lecturer has three speaking engagements this week.

definitions.definition: Connection or attachment.
definitions.example: Check the gears for full engagement before turning the handle.

definitions.definition: (by extension, about human emotional state) The feeling of being compelled, drawn in, connected to what is happening, interested in what will happen next.
The period of time when marriage is planned or promised.
definitions.example: We are enjoying a long engagement, but haven't yet set a date.

definitions.definition: In any situation of conflict, an actual instance of active hostilities.
definitions.example: The engagement resulted in many casualties.

definitions.definition: The point at which the fencers are close enough to join blades, or to make an effective attack during an encounter.
definitions.example: After engagement it quickly became clear which of the fencers was going to prevail.

definitions.definition: Dirty; shabby; in disrepair.
Of or relating to grunge music.
To make someone a victim or sacrifice.
To punish someone unjustly.
To swindle or defraud someone.
Having been made into a victim or treated like one.
The state of being dark; lack of light.
definitions.example: The darkness of the room made it difficult to see.

definitions.definition: Gloom.
The product of being dark.
The state or quality of reflecting little light, of tending to a blackish or brownish color.
definitions.example: The darkness of her skin betrayed her Mediterranean heritage.

definitions.definition: Evilness, lack of understanding or compassion, reference to death or suffering.
Power derived from the motion of a horse.
A non-metric unit of power (symbol hp) with various definitions, for different applications. The most common of them is probably the mechanical horsepower, approximately equal to 745.7 watts.
A metric unit (symbol often PS from the German abbreviation), approximately equal to 735.5 watts.
Strength.
definitions.example: political horsepower

definitions.definition: To fund a project; to underwrite something.
definitions.example: Professor Stebbins didn't need to apply for government grants because his lab was bankrolled by a large pharmaceutical corporation.

definitions.definition: The state of being kind.
An instance of kind or charitable behaviour.
definitions.example: How can I thank you for your many kindnesses?

definitions.definition: The act of founding, fixing, establishing, or beginning to erect.
definitions.example: The foundation of his institute has been wrought with difficulty.

definitions.definition: That upon which anything is founded; that on which anything stands, and by which it is supported; the lowest and supporting layer of a superstructure; underbuilding.
The result of the work to begin something; that which stabilizes and allows an enterprise or system to develop.
In solitaire or patience games, one of the piles of cards that the player attempts to build, usually holding all cards of a suit in ascending order.
The lowest and supporting part or member of a wall, including the base course and footing courses; in a frame house, the whole substructure of masonry.
definitions.example: The foundations of this construction have been laid out.

definitions.definition: A donation or legacy appropriated to support a charitable institution, and constituting a permanent fund; endowment.
That which is founded, or established by endowment; an endowed institution or charity.
definitions.example: The Wikimedia Foundation, Inc. is the parent organization of the Wiktionary collaborative project.

definitions.definition: Cosmetic cream roughly skin-colored, designed to make the face appear uniform in color and texture.
A basis for social bodies or intellectual disciplines.
Something that gives a pricking sensation; a sharp object.
Covered with sharp points.
definitions.example: The prickly pear is a cactus; you have to peel it before eating it to remove the spines and the tough skin.

definitions.definition: Easily irritated.
definitions.example: He has a prickly personality. He doesn't get along with people because he is easily set off.

definitions.definition: Difficult; complicated; (figuratively) hairy or thorny.
definitions.example: It was a prickly situation.

definitions.definition: In a prickly manner.
A small, fast motorboat.

To show mirth, satisfaction, or derision, by peculiar movement of the muscles of the face, particularly of the mouth, causing a lighting up of the face and eyes, and usually accompanied by the emission of explosive or chuckling sounds from the chest and throat; to indulge in laughter.
definitions.example: There were many laughing children running on the school grounds.

definitions.definition: To be or appear cheerful, pleasant, mirthful, lively, or brilliant; to sparkle; to sport.
(followed by "at") To make an object of laughter or ridicule; to make fun of; to deride; to mock.
definitions.example: Don't laugh at my new hat, man!

definitions.definition: To affect or influence by means of laughter or ridicule.
To express by, or utter with, laughter.
An upward grade or slope.
An improved component or replacement item, usually applied to technology
definitions.example: With my phone company, I get a free upgrade every twelve months if I keep topping up 10 pounds a month.

definitions.definition: An improvement
definitions.example: The remarried couples among my friends often joked that their divorce and remarriage amounted to an upgrade.

definitions.definition: To improve, usually applied to technology, generally by complete replacement of one or more components
definitions.example: When you upgrade your standard of living, you become addicted to that standard of living.

definitions.definition: To replace with something better.
definitions.example: I had to upgrade my anti-virus software to protect my computer from the newer threats.

definitions.definition: To improve the equipment or furnishings of or services rendered to
definitions.example: They upgraded him to the latest model.

definitions.definition: To improve in condition or status.
definitions.example: She upgraded to a more successful husband.

definitions.definition: To replace a program with a later version of itself, a version having a higher version number or marketed under a more recent product name.
Dissatisfied and irritable.
definitions.example: The old man was grumpy because he couldn’t find his glasses.

definitions.definition: An airfield (an area designated for the takeoff and landing of aircraft), including one or more runways and (for commercial airports) one or more passenger terminals.
definitions.example: Because of this, and contrary to popular belief, British airports primarily act as giant wealth extractors, transporting our richest citizens out of the country as often as possible to take their money overseas. ― The Independent, Heathrow expansion to mainly benefit wealthy frequent fliers, research finds, 23 june 2018.

definitions.definition: An earthenware container, either with two or no handles, for holding oil, water, wine, etc., or used for burial.
A small, approximately cylindrical container, normally made of clay or glass, for holding fruit, preserves, etc., or for ornamental purposes.
A jar and its contents; as much as fills such a container; a jarful.
To preserve (food) in a jar.
A clashing or discordant set of sounds, particularly with a quivering or vibrating quality.
A quivering or vibrating movement or sensation resulting from something being shaken or struck.
(by extension) A sense of alarm or dismay.
The effect of something contradictory or discordant; a clash.
A disagreement, a dispute, a quarrel; contention, discord; quarrelling.
To knock, shake, or strike sharply, especially causing a quivering or vibrating movement.
definitions.example: He hit it with a hammer, hoping he could jar it loose.

definitions.definition: To harm or injure by such action.
To shock or surprise.
definitions.example: I think the accident jarred him, as he hasn’t got back in a car since.

definitions.definition: To act in disagreement or opposition, to clash, to be at odds with; to interfere; to dispute, to quarrel.
To (cause something to) give forth a rudely tremulous or quivering sound; to (cause something to) sound discordantly or harshly.
definitions.example: The clashing notes jarred on my ears.

definitions.definition: To quiver or vibrate due to being shaken or struck.
Of the appearance, form, style, etc., of people and things: to look strangely different; to stand out awkwardly from its surroundings; to be incongruent.
To celebrate victory with pomp; to rejoice over success; to exult in an advantage gained; to exhibit exultation.
To prevail over rivals, challenges, or difficulties.
To succeed, win, or attain ascendancy.
To be prosperous; to flourish.
To play a trump in a card game.
Freedom from some business or activity.
Free time given over to a specific purpose; occupation, activity.
A period during which official activity or business is formally suspended; an official holiday from university, law courts etc.
A holiday; a stretch of leisure time away from work or duty and devoted to rest or pleasure.
The act of vacating something; moving out.
definitions.example: The Conservative Party’s vacation of the centre ground gave an opportunity to its opponents.

definitions.definition: The act of making legally void.
To spend or take a vacation.
definitions.example: This year, we’re vacationing in Mexico.

definitions.definition: To draw air into the lungs, through the nose or mouth by action of the diaphragm.

To draw air or any form of gas (either in a pure form, or mixed with small particles in form of aerosols/smoke -sometimes stemming from a medicament) into the lungs, through the nose or mouth by action of the diaphragm.
To eat very quickly.
A weight training implement consisting of a short bar with weight counterpoised on each end.
A stupid person.
A covering for the head, often in the approximate form of a cone or a cylinder closed at its top end, and sometimes having a brim and other decoration.
A particular role or capacity that a person might fill.
Any receptacle from which numbers/names are pulled out in a lottery.
A hat switch.
The háček symbol.
The caret symbol ^.
User rights on a website, such as the right to edit pages others cannot.
(Cambridge University slang) A student who is also the son of a nobleman (and so allowed to wear a hat instead of a mortarboard).
To place a hat on.
To appoint as cardinal.
An eating establishment in which diners are served food, usually by waiters at their tables but sometimes (as in a fast food restaurant) at a counter.
A room in a house where a bed is kept for sleeping.
definitions.example: Please don't enter my bedroom without knocking.

definitions.definition: Mythical; existing in myth.
Larger-than-life.

A person who flees or escapes and travels secretly from place to place, and sometimes using disguises and aliases to conceal his/her identity, as to avoid law authorities in order to avoid an arrest or prosecution; or to avoid some other unwanted situation.
Fleeing or running away
Transient, fleeting or ephemeral
Elusive or difficult to retain
Facilities for urination and defecation: a toilet; a lavatory.
The fact of being easy, or easily done; absence of difficulty, simplicity.
Dexterity of speech or action; skill, talent.
definitions.example: The facility she shows in playing the violin is unrivalled.

definitions.definition: The physical means or contrivances to make something (especially a public service) possible; the required equipment, infrastructure, location etc.
definitions.example: Transport facilities in Bangkok are not sufficient to prevent frequent traffic collapses during rush hour.

definitions.definition: An institution specially designed for a specific purpose, such as incarceration, military use, or scientific experimentation.
(in the plural) A toilet.
A condition of mental weakness less than idiocy, but enough to make a person easily persuaded to do something against their better interest.
Affability.
To make a solemn promise (to do something).
To deposit something as a security; to pawn.
To give assurance of friendship by the act of drinking; to drink to one's health.
Cloth-covered frame used for protection against rain or sun.
definitions.example: Quick, grab that umbrella before you get rained on!

definitions.definition: Generally, anything that provides protection.
definitions.example: The fighters provide a defensive air umbrella over the battle group.

definitions.definition: Something that covers a wide range of concepts, purposes, groups, etc.
definitions.example: The test facility was established under the umbrella of the company's quality program.

definitions.definition: The main body of a jellyfish, excluding the tentacles.
definitions.example: Jellyfish are composed of more than 90% water and most of their umbrella mass is made up of gelatinous material.

definitions.definition: An umbrella-shaped reflector with a white or silvery inner surface, used to diffuse a nearby light.
To cover or protect, as if by an umbrella.
To form the dome shape of an open umbrella.
To move like a jellyfish.
One who makes observations, monitors or takes notice
definitions.example: Most impartial observers agreed that Sampras had not served well.

definitions.definition: One who adheres or follows laws, guidelines, etc.
definitions.example: I shall be an observer of the local customs.

definitions.definition: A person sent as a representative, to a meeting or other function to monitor but not to participate
definitions.example: The UN sent many observers to the country's first elections.

definitions.definition: A country or other entity which has limited participation rights within an organization.
definitions.example: The Vatican and Palestine have observer status at the UN.

definitions.definition: A crew member on an aircraft who makes observations of enemy positions or aircraft
definitions.example: The only crew-member to survive the crash was the Canadian observer.

definitions.definition: A sentry etc. manning an observation post
definitions.example: We waited till dusk when the observers' vision was poorest.

definitions.definition: To perform a work or labour; to exert power or strength, physical or mechanical; to act.
To produce an appropriate physical effect; to issue in the result designed by nature; especially to take appropriate effect on the human system.
To act or produce effect on the mind; to exert moral power or influence.
To perform some manual act upon a human body in a methodical manner, and usually with instruments, with a view to restore soundness or health, as in amputation, lithotomy, etc.
To deal in stocks or any commodity with a view to speculative profits.
To produce, as an effect; to cause.
To put into, or to continue in, operation or activity; to work.
definitions.example: to operate a machine

definitions.definition: (in combination) operated by the means specified e.g. a battery-operated toy
Having undergone an operation
Nonsense; meaningless or encrypted language.
Something written in an overly complex, incoherent, or incomprehensible manner.
Having greed; consumed by selfish desires.
Prone to overeat.
Tending to match as much text as possible.
definitions.example: This regular expression performs a greedy match.

definitions.definition: (of an algorithm) That tries to find the global optimum by finding the local optimum at each stage.
A person who jogs (as exercise).
A tracksuit, particularly the trousers.
A printing press operator who removes, jogs, and stacks the sheets or signatures of paper.
(chiefly derogatory) A black person.
A tracksuit, particularly the trousers, a jogger.
A container for ink, designed and usually positioned so that a person may conveniently dip a pen into it whenever a refill is needed.
The state of being difficult, or hard to do.
An obstacle that hinders achievement of a goal.
definitions.example: We faced a difficulty in trying to book a flight so late.

definitions.definition: (sometimes in the plural) Physical danger from the environment, especially with risk of drowning
definitions.example: 2012 August 2, "Children rescued after getting into difficulties in Donegal" BBC Online

definitions.definition: An objection.
That which cannot be easily understood or believed.
An awkward situation or quarrel.
The act, process, or state of reinforcing or being reinforced.
A thing that reinforces.
(in the plural) Additional troops or materiel sent to support a military action.
The process whereby a behavior with desirable consequences comes to be repeated.
Grieved and disappointed, especially with the loss of a beloved person or thing, such as the repudiation of a romantic relationship.
The opening kick of each half of a game of football.
(by extension) the opening sequence of any event
A screen with a wider aspect ratio than the ordinary 35-millimeter frame, making more effective use of the human field of view and producing a more immersive view experience.
Filmed in a greater aspect ratio than the 1.33:1 or 1.37:1 aspect ratio.
(of a motion picture) Presented in the original aspect ratio; presented in letterbox orientation.
An instrument of attack or defense in combat or hunting, e.g. most guns, missiles, or swords.
definitions.example: The club that is now mostly used for golf was once a common weapon.

definitions.definition: An instrument or other means of harming or exerting control over another.
definitions.example: Money is the main weapon of modern oligarchs.

definitions.definition: A tool of any kind.
definitions.example: Choose your weapon.

definitions.definition: An idiot, an oaf, a fool, a tool; a contemptible or incompetent person.
A person who writes, or produces literary work.
definitions.example: Has your girlfriend written you another letter already? She’s quite a writer!

definitions.definition: Anything that writes or produces output.
The seller of an option.
A clerk of a certain rank in the service of the East India Company, who, after serving a certain number of years, became a factor.
An ordinary legal practitioner in Scottish country towns.
A petty officer in the United States navy who keeps the watch-muster and other books of the ship.
A percussive keyboard musical instrument, usually ranging over seven octaves, with white and black colored keys, played by pressing these keys, causing hammers to strike strings
definitions.example: He can play "Happy Birthday" on the piano.

definitions.definition: Soft, quiet
In extended use; quiet, subdued
Ability to produce a desired effect under ideal testing conditions.
Degree of ability to produce a desired effect.
A system for transferring messages from one computer to another, usually through a network.
definitions.example: He sent me his details via email.

definitions.definition: A quantity of messages sent through an email system.
definitions.example: I am searching through my old email.

definitions.definition: A message sent through an email system.
definitions.example: He sent me an email last week to remind me about the meeting.

definitions.definition: An email address.
definitions.example: Don’t send personal messages to my work email.

definitions.definition: To send an email or emails to.
definitions.example: She emailed me last week, asking about the status of the project.

definitions.definition: (may take two objects) To send (something) through email.
definitions.example: He emailed the file out to everyone.

definitions.definition: To send, or compose and send, an email or emails.
definitions.example: Most teenagers seem to spend almost the whole day emailing and surfing the Web.

definitions.definition: To bring together as one.
definitions.example: I hope this song can unite people from all different cultures.

definitions.definition: To come together as one.
definitions.example: If we want to win, we will need to unite.

definitions.definition: Joined into a single entity.
Involving the joint activity of multiple agents.
A garden or an area of land for the cultivation of fruit or nut trees.
The trees themselves cultivated in such an area.
The act of offending:
The state of being offended or displeased; anger; displeasure.
A strategy and tactics employed when in position to score; contrasted with defense.
The portion of a team dedicated to scoring when in position to do so; contrasted with defense.
A child of someone's child.
Understood by only a few.
definitions.example: arcane rituals

definitions.definition: (by extension) Obscure, mysterious.
definitions.example: arcane details

definitions.definition: Requiring secret or mysterious knowledge to understand.
Extremely old (e.g. interpretation or knowledge), and possibly irrelevant.
definitions.example: An arcane law

definitions.definition: A short spar, at the bows of a ship, that serves as a flagpole to fly a flag (a jack)
Jagged, toothed or serrated
A canopy over a bed.
Something that overhangs something else; especially a canopy or soundboard over a pulpit.
That which is vain, futile, or worthless; that which is of no value, use or profit.
Excessive pride in or admiration of one's own abilities, appearance or achievements.

A dressing table used to apply makeup, preen, and coif hair. The table is normally quite low and similar to a desk, with drawers and one or more mirrors on top. Either a chair or bench is used to sit upon.
A washbasin installed into a permanently fixed storage unit, used as an item of bathroom furniture.
Emptiness.
Any idea, theory or statement that is without foundation.
definitions.example: It is a vanity to say that if two stones are dropped from a tower, the heavier will experience the greater acceleration.

definitions.definition: A bitter, destructive feud, normally between two families, clans or factions, in which each injury or slaying is revenged: a blood feud.
(often preceded by personal) A motivational grudge against a person or faction, which may or may not be reciprocated; the state of having it in for someone.
A line or vector that is perpendicular to another line, surface, or plane.
A person who is normal, who fits into mainstream society, as opposed to those who live alternative lifestyles.
The usual state.
definitions.example: Heavy workload is the new normal.

definitions.definition: According to norms or rules or to a regular pattern.
definitions.example: Organize the data into third normal form.

definitions.definition: Usual, healthy; not sick or ill or unlike oneself.
definitions.example: John is feeling normal again.

definitions.definition: (of a school) teaching teachers how to teach (to certain norms)
definitions.example: My grandmother attended Mankato State Normal School.

definitions.definition: Of, relating to, or being a solution containing one equivalent weight of solute per litre of solution.
Describing a straight chain isomer of an aliphatic hydrocarbon, or an aliphatic compound in which a substituent is in the 1- position of such a hydrocarbon.
(of a mode in an oscillating system) In which all parts of an object vibrate at the same frequency (see normal mode).
(of points) In the default position, set for the most frequently used route.
Perpendicular to a tangent of a curve or derivative of a surface.
definitions.example: The interior normal vector of an ideal perfect sphere will always point toward the center, and the exterior normal vector directly away, and both will always be co-linear with the ray whose' tip ends at the point of intersection, which is the intersection of all three sets of points.

definitions.definition: Any fibre having a nanoscale diameter
Unstable; easily rocked.
definitions.example: The table was rocky, so we put a book under one leg.

definitions.definition: In the style of rock music.
definitions.example: His new album is quite rocky.

definitions.definition: Troubled; or difficult; in danger or distress.
definitions.example: Their relationship had weathered some rocky times, but they loved each other.

definitions.definition: (Irish folklore) A female spirit, usually taking the form of a woman whose mournful wailing warns of an impending death.
A noisy or ill-tempered woman.
The act or instance of wandering.
definitions.example: to go for a wander in the park

definitions.definition: The situation where a value or signal etc. deviates from the correct or normal value.
definitions.example: baseline wander in ECG signals

definitions.definition: To move without purpose or specified destination; often in search of livelihood.
definitions.example: to wander over the fields

definitions.definition: To stray; stray from one's course; err.
definitions.example: A writer wanders from his subject.

definitions.definition: To commit adultery.
To go somewhere indirectly or at varying speeds; to move in a curved path.
Of the mind, to lose focus or clarity of argument or attention.
Any of flowering species of the genus Lycium.
Lycium barbarum or Lycium chinense
The berry of this plant.
A land area free of woodland, cities, and towns; open country.
definitions.example: There are several species of wild flowers growing in this field.

definitions.definition: A wide, open space that is usually used to grow crops or to hold farm animals.
definitions.example: A crop circle was made in a corn field.

definitions.definition: A place where competitive matches are carried out.
Any of various figurative meanings, regularly dead metaphors.
To intercept or catch (a ball) and play it.
(and other batting sports) To be the team catching and throwing the ball, as opposed to hitting it.
definitions.example: The blue team are fielding first, while the reds are batting.

definitions.definition: To place (a team, its players, etc.) in a game.
definitions.example: The away team fielded two new players and the second-choice goalkeeper.

definitions.definition: To answer; to address.
definitions.example: She will field questions immediately after her presentation.

definitions.definition: To defeat.
definitions.example: They fielded a fearsome army.

definitions.definition: To execute research (in the field).
definitions.example: He fielded the marketing survey about the upcoming product.

definitions.definition: To deploy in the field.

definitions.definition: Any beetle of the family Lampyridae, which exhibit bioluminescence during twilight.
To bring peace to (a place or situation), by ending war, fighting, violence, anger or agitation.
To appease (someone).
In an official manner; according to official rules or regulations.
Thoroughly, completely.

definitions.definition: A small cucumber, often pickled whole.
The penis.
To feel shame; to be ashamed.
To make ashamed; to shame.
Feeling shame or guilt.
Of or relating to theory; abstract; not empirical.
